latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
29 Jun 2026 10:38
RNS Number : 1679K
Creo Medical Group PLC
29 June 2026
Creo Medical Group plc
("Creo", the "Company" or the "Group")
Result of AGM
Creo Medical Group plc (AIM: CREO), a medical device company focused on the emerging field of minimally invasive surgical endoscopy for pre-cancer and cancer patients,
announces
that all resolutions put to shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting held earlier today were duly passed.
The voting results will be made available on the Investor Relations section of the Company's website shortly:
https://www.creomedical.com/en/investors/reports-and-presentations
For further information please contact:
Creo Medical Group plc
www.creomedical.com
Richard Craven, Company Secretary
Via Walbrook PR
Shore Capital (Nominated Adviser and Corporate Broker)
Daniel Bush / Lucy Bowden
+44 (0)20 7408 4090
Walbrook PR Ltd
Tel: +44 (0)20 7933 8780 or
creo@walbrookpr.com
Paul McManus / Alice Woodings
Mob: +44 (0)7980 541 893 /
+44 (0)7407 804 654
About Creo Medical
Creo is a medical device company focused on the development and commercialisation of minimally invasive electrosurgical devices, bringing advanced energy to endoscopy.
The Company's vision is to improve patient outcomes through the development and commercialisation of a suite of electrosurgical medical devices, each enabled by CROMA, powered by Kamaptive. The Group has developed the CROMA powered by Kamaptive full-spectrum adaptive technology to optimise surgical capability and patient outcomes. Kamaptive is a seamless, intuitive integration of multi-modal energy sources, optimised to dynamically adapt to patient tissue during procedures such as resection, dissection, coagulation, and ablation of tissue. Kamaptive technology provides clinicians with increased flexibility, precision and controlled surgical solutions. CROMA currently delivers bipolar radiofrequency ("RF") energy for precise localised cutting and focused high frequency microwave ("MW") energy for controlled coagulation and ablation via a single accessory port. This technology, combined with the Group's range of patented electrosurgical devices, is designed to provide clinicians with flexible, accurate and controlled clinical solutions. The Directors believe the Company's technology can impact the landscape of surgery and endoscopy by providing a safer, less invasive and more cost-efficient option for procedures.
For more information, please refer to the website
www.creomedical.com

latest.title: AGM Statement
latest.content:
29 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0269K
Creo Medical Group PLC
29 June 2026
Creo Medical Group plc
("Creo", the "Company" or the "Group")
AGM Statement
Creo Medical Group plc (AIM: CREO), the medical device company focused on the emerging field of minimally invasive surgical endoscopy for pre-cancer and cancer patients, provides an update to shareholders ahead of its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") being held today
at the offices of Osborne Clarke LLP at Halo, Counterslip, Bristol BS1 6AJ.
Addressing shareholders, Kevin T. Crofton, Non-Executive Chairman
, will say:
"
As previously reported, the FY25 growth momentum has continued into FY26 with strong trading performance in the first three months of the current financial year. Year-on-year revenue growth (Q1 FY26 vs Q1 FY25) was approximately 60%, at the higher end of management's expectations, reflecting continued customer adoption, growing procedural volumes and further conversion of the Group's commercial pipeline into revenue across our key markets. As set out in our FY25 Results, this strong performance underpins the Board's confidence in the ongoing growth of the business and we believe that full year FY26 revenue growth will be between 50% and 60% compared to FY25 (previous guidance was between 40% and 60% growth).
"Recent clinical and commercial updates have also demonstrated the breadth of opportunity across our core range of products. Since April, the Group has announced further evidence of adoption and engagement, including: Speedboat, the first use of Speedboat in a UK private hospital as well as a key opinion leader's presentation on Creo's core products at Digestive Disease Week; SpydrBlade Flex, highlighting the use of SpydrBlade Flex in novel BEAM procedures; and a commercial update on MicroBlate Fine. Together, these developments reinforce the increasing relevance of our products to clinicians and healthcare providers in our target markets.
"Alongside this commercial progress, we remain focussed on improving the operational efficiency and financial discipline of the business. On 16 April 2026 we announced the sale and outsourcing of our manufacturing operations, which we expect to reduce annual operating costs by over £1.0m, representing a further 15% reduction from the FY25 closing run rate on a pro forma basis, whilst supporting continuity of supply and manufacturing quality.
"Given the scale of the near-term growth opportunity, we have also taken steps to strengthen the Group's balance sheet and support the next phase of commercial execution. The recently completed placing raised gross proceeds of £5.5 million, with Directors subscribing for £2.15 million in the placing. We also announced the constitution of a convertible loan note instrument and the conditional subscription for, in aggregate, £2.0 million (before expenses) of loan notes by Development Bank of Wales which completed simultaneously with the funding round.
"The proceeds will support inventory, customer roll-out and commercial expansion at a point of accelerating demand. Together with the expected benefits of the manufacturing outsourcing and the proposed sale of our remaining 49% interest in Creo Medical Europe, subject to completion, we believe these actions provide the Group with sufficient cash resources to fund its development towards to sustainable cash flow generation and profitability.
"Creo enters the remainder of 2026 with strong commercial momentum, strengthened foundations and a more focussed operating model. On behalf of the Board, I thank our shareholders for their continued support as we continue to execute against our strategy and build long term value."
For further information please contact:
Creo Medical Group plc
www.creomedical.com
Richard Craven, Company Secretary
Via Walbrook PR
Shore Capital (Nominated Adviser and Corporate Broker)

The Company's vision is to improve patient outcomes through the development and commercialisation of a suite of electrosurgical medical devices, each enabled by CROMA, powered by Kamaptive. The Group has developed the CROMA powered by Kamaptive full-spectrum adaptive technology to optimise surgical capability and patient outcomes. Kamaptive is a seamless, intuitive integration of multi-modal energy sources, optimised to dynamically adapt to patient tissue during procedures such as resection, dissection, coagulation, and ablation of tissue. Kamaptive technology provides clinicians with increased flexibility, precision and controlled surgical solutions. CROMA currently delivers bipolar radiofrequency ("RF") energy for precise localised cutting and focused high frequency microwave ("MW") energy for controlled coagulation and ablation via a single accessory port. This technology, combined with the Group's range of patented electrosurgical devices, is designed to provide clinicians with flexible, accurate and controlled clinical solutions. The Directors believe the Company's technology can impact the landscape of surgery and endoscopy by providing a safer, less invasive and more cost-efficient option for procedures.

latest.title: Posting of 2025 Annual Report and Notice of AGM
latest.content:
4 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8875G
Creo Medical Group PLC
04 June 2026
Creo Medical Group plc
("Creo", the "Company" or the "Group")
Posting of 2025 Annual Report and Accounts
and
Notice of Annual General Meeting
Creo Medical Group plc (AIM: CREO), the medical device company specialising in advanced energy solutions for therapeutic endoscopy, announces that the Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the Notice of the Company's Annual General Meeting ("AGM") have now been published on the Company's website at
https://www.creomedical.com/en/investors/reports-and-presentations
. Hard copies have been posted to those shareholders who have not elected to receive electronic communication from the Company.
The AGM will be held on 29 June 2026 at 10:00 a.m. at the offices of Osborne Clarke LLP at Halo, Counterslip, Bristol BS1 6AJ, and will consider the Resolutions set out in the Notice of AGM. The Company's CEO, Craig Gulliford, will make a short presentation at the meeting. No new material information will be included within the presentation.
Shareholders are encouraged to vote by proxy at the AGM, which can be done:
·
by completing, signing the Form of Proxy and returning it to Equiniti; or
·
in the case of CREST members, by utilising the CREST electronic proxy appointment service.
Proxy votes must be received no later than 10:00 a.m. on 25 June 2026. All voting at the AGM will be conducted by a poll. Shareholders are recommended to appoint the Chair of the meeting as their proxy rather than a named person, as any such other person may not be permitted to attend the AGM in the event of unforeseen circumstances (e.g. illness).
For further information please contact:
Creo Medical Group plc
www.creomedical.com
Richard Craven, Company Secretary
Via Walbrook PR

company_30s.what_they_do: Creo Ltd. designs and sells advanced surgical instruments – from the MicroBlate™ fine‑cutting device to the SpydrBlade™ Flex used in novel beam‑guided procedures, and the Speedboat system for targeted cancer treatment. The focus is on minimally invasive, precision‑driven surgery.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The market for precision surgical tools is growing as hospitals shift to less invasive techniques. Creo’s products target high‑margin oncology and specialty surgery segments where demand for safer, more effective instruments is strong and competition is limited to a handful of niche players.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a restructuring phase: it has just announced a £5.5m placing, a 49 % sale of its Spanish subsidiary, and a move to outsource manufacturing to cut costs. FY25 results showed a modest revenue lift but continued cash burn. The stock is trading near its lowest levels since the 2024 peak, and the latest AGM will decide on conversion terms for new debt and the sale proceeds.

rns_narrative: Corporate actions are front‑and‑center – the AGM will set conversion prices for new debt and outline the sale of Creo Medical SL, while a fresh £5.5m placement aims to shore up cash.
Product momentum is visible – MicroBlate and SpydrBlade updates signal that the devices are moving through early‑adopter trials, and Speedboat is already being used in a private hospital cancer case.
Financial discipline is tightening – FY25 results and a Q1 trading update show revenue growth but also a strategic outsourcing of manufacturing to reduce overheads.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: A quiet but cautious optimism – traders are watching the AGM and placement for a potential turnaround.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Senator1 and a few insiders highlight that the conversion terms could unlock upside, and the sale of the Spanish stake should bring in cash for R&D.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the new debt and dilution from the placing could worsen cash burn, while the sale of a major asset may signal a lack of confidence in the core business.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Earlier chatter was largely negative, but recent posts suggest a shift toward monitoring the AGM outcome and the success of the new financing round.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Creo is flat – no public search activity, which means any news or earnings surprise could hit hard.

near_term_catalysts: Positive AGM outcome – favourable conversion terms or a clear plan to use the proceeds.
Closing of the £5.5m placement – an influx of cash that could fund marketing or product rollout.
First commercial sales or regulatory clearance for MicroBlate or SpydrBlade.
A spike in trading volume that confirms institutional interest.
A supportive price action at the recent low, signalling the start of a reversal.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 10–15 % volume uptick coupled with a bounce off the 20‑day moving average. A press release announcing a new hospital contract or a regulatory nod would also be a clear trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑capitulation recovery – the APEX compression score is moderate, suggesting a spring that could snap once a catalyst arrives. Confirmation will come from a sustained move above the last trough and a volume spike.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is 70–95 % below its all‑time high, meaning a rebound to just half of that peak would deliver a 1.5‑to‑3‑fold gain. Historical deep‑crash signals have returned 40–60 % on average, and the company has tangible catalysts that could unlock value. The asymmetry is clear: a small loss if the bounce fails, but the upside could be several times the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the AGM fails to produce favourable conversion terms, the placement closes at a lower price, or the new debt dilutes existing shareholders, the stock could fall further. Regulatory delays or failure to secure commercial contracts would also keep the price stuck.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price (0 % if it stays flat). Potential gain ranges from 1.5× to 3× if it recovers to 50 % of its peak, giving a risk‑to‑reward ratio of roughly 1:3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the price closes above the 20‑day moving average with at least a 5‑point bounce off the recent low. Size the position so that a 5‑point drop would wipe out the trade, but a 15‑point rise would double the account. Place a stop 3 points below the low.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the AGM results or the closing of the placement. If either event is positive, consider a quick entry. If the placement closes below the AGM‑price target, stay out.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy warnings. The only terminal risk would be a total collapse of the product pipeline, which is unlikely given the current contract and trial activity.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cri­o is a deep‑crashed, precision‑surgery specialist that just secured a new cash injection and a product‑pipeline boost – a classic contrarian play with high upside if the AGM and placement unlock value.
